message:
✨ Add 8 CNCF install missions (final batch consolidation) (#293)

Cherry-picked mission files from batch runner completion:
- piraeus-datastore, ratify, security-compliance, sermant
- spinkube, tremor, volcano, wasmedge-runtime

Total install missions: 186 (178 existing + 8 new)
Index regenerated with 350 total entries.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,147 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-piraeus-datastore",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Piraeus Datastore on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "The Piraeus Operator manages LINSTOR clusters in Kubernetes, providing a robust solution for software-defined storage. Installing it allows you to easily deploy and manage storage resources in your Kubernetes environment.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Add the Piraeus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:\n```bash\nhelm repo add piraeus https://charts.piraeus.io\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install the Piraeus Operator",
+        "description": "Install the Piraeus Operator using Helm with the specified version:\n```bash\nhelm install piraeus piraeus/piraeus --version 2.10.4 --namespace piraeus-datastore --create-namespace\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check if the Piraeus Operator pod is running:\n```bash\nkubectl wait pod --for=condition=Ready -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/component=piraeus-operator\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Create a LINSTOR cluster",
+        "description": "Deploy a basic LINSTOR cluster by applying the following manifest:\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f - <<EOF\napiVersion: piraeus.io/v1\nkind: LinstorCluster\nmetadata:\n  name: linstorcluster\nspec: {}\nEOF\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits",
+        "description": "Edit the Helm release to set resource limits for the operator:\n```bash\nhelm upgrade piraeus piraeus/piraeus --namespace piraeus-datastore --set operator.resources.limits.cpu=100m --set operator.resources.limits.memory=128Mi\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Port-forward to access the operator",
+        "description": "Set up port-forwarding to access the operator's API:\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/piraeus-operator-controller-manager -n piraeus-datastore 8080:443\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
+        "description": "Uninstall the Piraeus Operator release:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall piraeus --namespace piraeus-datastore\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up CRDs",
+        "description": "Delete the Custom Resource Definitions associated with the Piraeus Operator:\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd linstorclusters.piraeus.io\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the namespace",
+        "description": "Delete the namespace used for Piraeus Operator:\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace piraeus-datastore\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Backup current state",
+        "description": "Export the current LINSTOR cluster configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get linstorcluster -n piraeus-datastore -o yaml > linstorcluster-backup.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Update the Piraeus Helm repository to get the latest charts:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade the Piraeus Operator",
+        "description": "Upgrade the Piraeus Operator to the latest version:\n```bash\nhelm upgrade piraeus piraeus/piraeus --namespace piraeus-datastore --version 2.10.4\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
+        "description": "Check if the upgraded Piraeus Operator pod is running:\n```bash\nkubectl wait pod --for=condition=Ready -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/component=piraeus-operator\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff",
+        "description": "This may indicate a configuration issue. Check the logs of the pod:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -n piraeus-datastore <pod-name>\n```\nLook for error messages that indicate what went wrong."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Service not reachable",
+        "description": "If the service is not reachable, ensure that the service is running:\n```bash\nkubectl get svc -n piraeus-datastore\n```\nCheck the endpoints:\n```bash\nkubectl get endpoints -n piraeus-datastore\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Failed to create LinstorCluster",
+        "description": "If the LinstorCluster fails to create, check the events:\n```bash\nkubectl describe linstorcluster linstorcluster -n piraeus-datastore\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Insufficient resources",
+        "description": "If pods are not starting due to resource limits, check the resource usage:\n```bash\nkubectl top pods -n piraeus-datastore\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation of the Piraeus Operator will have the operator pod running in the 'piraeus-datastore' namespace, and you will be able to create a LINSTOR cluster. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring no errors are present in the logs.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add piraeus https://charts.piraeus.io\nhelm repo update",
+        "helm install piraeus piraeus/piraeus --version 2.10.4 --namespace piraeus-datastore --create-namespace",
+        "kubectl wait pod --for=condition=Ready -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/component=piraeus-operator",
+        "kubectl apply -f - <<EOF\napiVersion: piraeus.io/v1\nkind: LinstorCluster\nmetadata:\n  name: linstorcluster\nspec: {}\nEOF",
+        "helm upgrade piraeus piraeus/piraeus --namespace piraeus-datastore --set operator.resources.limits.cpu=100m --set operator.resources.limits.memory=128Mi"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "storage",
+      "sandbox"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "piraeus-datastore"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "projectVersion": "v2.10.4",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "quay.io/piraeusdatastore/piraeus-operator:v2.10.4"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://piraeus.io/",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/piraeusdatastore/piraeus-operator",
+      "helm": "https://github.com/piraeusdatastore/piraeus-operator/tree/main/charts/piraeus/"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and Helm installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-02T22:35:14.264Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-install-gen-1.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,134 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-ratify",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Ratify on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Ratify is an Artifact Ratification Framework that enables verification of artifact security metadata, ensuring that only compliant artifacts are deployed. Installing Ratify helps organizations enforce security policies and improve their deployment processes.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Add the Ratify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.\n```bash\nhelm repo add ratify https://charts.ratify.dev\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install Ratify",
+        "description": "Install Ratify using Helm in a new namespace called 'ratify'.\n```bash\nhelm install ratify ratify/ratify --namespace ratify --create-namespace --version v1.4.0\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check if the Ratify pods are running successfully.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace ratify\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits",
+        "description": "Set resource limits for the Ratify deployment to ensure it operates within defined constraints.\n```bash\nkubectl patch deployment ratify --namespace ratify --type='json' -p='[{\"op\": \"add\", \"path\": \"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources\", \"value\": {\"limits\": {\"cpu\": \"500m\", \"memory\": \"512Mi\"}, \"requests\": {\"cpu\": \"250m\", \"memory\": \"256Mi\"}}}]'\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
+        "description": "Uninstall the Ratify Helm release from the cluster.\n```bash\nhelm uninstall ratify --namespace ratify\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up CRDs",
+        "description": "Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were installed with Ratify.\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd ratifyconfigs.ratify.dev\nkubectl delete crd ratifypolicies.ratify.dev\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the namespace",
+        "description": "Delete the 'ratify' namespace to clean up all resources associated with Ratify.\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace ratify\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Backup current state",
+        "description": "Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of Ratify.\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace ratify -o yaml > ratify-backup.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Ratify",
+        "description": "Upgrade the Ratify installation to the latest version.\n```bash\nhelm upgrade ratify ratify/ratify --namespace ratify --version v1.4.0\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
+        "description": "Check the status of the Ratify pods to ensure they are running the new version.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace ratify\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff",
+        "description": "If the Ratify pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.\n```bash\nkubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace ratify\n``` \nLook for configuration issues or missing dependencies in the logs."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Helm release not found",
+        "description": "If you encounter an error stating the Helm release is not found, ensure you are using the correct namespace.\n```bash\nhelm list --namespace ratify\n``` \nIf the release is not listed, it may not have been installed correctly."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Resource limits causing OOMKilled",
+        "description": "If the pods are being killed due to Out Of Memory (OOM), consider increasing the memory limits set during configuration.\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace ratify\n``` \nAdjust the resource limits accordingly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation of Ratify will result in the Ratify pods running in the 'ratify' namespace without errors. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring they are in the 'Running' state.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add ratify https://charts.ratify.dev\nhelm repo update",
+        "helm install ratify ratify/ratify --namespace ratify --create-namespace --version v1.4.0",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace ratify",
+        "kubectl patch deployment ratify --namespace ratify --type='json' -p='[{\"op\": \"add\", \"path\": \"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources\", \"value\": {\"limits\": {\"cpu\": \"500m\", \"memory\": \"512Mi\"}, \"requests\": {\"cpu\": \"250m\", \"memory\": \"256Mi\"}}}]'"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "app-definition",
+      "sandbox"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "ratify"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Deployment",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "projectVersion": "v1.4.0",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "gcr.io/ratify-project/ratify:v1.4.0"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://ratify.dev",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/ratify-project/ratify"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-02T22:40:49.069Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-install-gen-1.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-security-compliance",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Security Compliance on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Zot is a production-ready vendor-neutral OCI image registry that allows you to store and manage container images in a compliant manner. Installing Zot will enable you to have a reliable and scalable solution for your container image storage needs.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the Zot Helm repository",
+        "description": "Add the official Zot Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:\n```bash\nhelm repo add zot https://zotregistry.dev/charts\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install Zot using Helm",
+        "description": "Install Zot in a new namespace called 'zot' with the specified version:\n```bash\nhelm install zot zot/zot --version v2.1.14 --namespace zot --create-namespace\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that the Zot pods are running:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace zot\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits",
+        "description": "Edit the Zot deployment to set resource limits:\n```bash\nkubectl edit deployment zot --namespace zot\n# Add the following under spec.containers:\n# resources:\n#   limits:\n#     cpu: \"500m\"\n#     memory: \"512Mi\"\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Port-forward to access the Zot service",
+        "description": "Set up port forwarding to access the Zot service locally:\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward service/zot --namespace zot 8080:8080\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
+        "description": "Uninstall the Zot Helm release from the cluster:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall zot --namespace zot\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the Zot namespace",
+        "description": "Remove the Zot namespace to clean up all associated resources:\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace zot\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Backup current state",
+        "description": "Before upgrading, it's a good practice to backup the current configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace zot -o yaml > zot-backup.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Zot to the latest version",
+        "description": "Upgrade the Zot installation to the latest version:\n```bash\nhelm upgrade zot zot/zot --version v2.1.14 --namespace zot\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
+        "description": "Check that the Zot pods are running after the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace zot\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pods stuck in Pending state",
+        "description": "If your pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability:\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace zot\n# Look for events indicating insufficient resources or scheduling issues.\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Zot service not accessible",
+        "description": "If you cannot access the Zot service, verify the port-forwarding command:\n```bash\nkubectl get svc --namespace zot\n# Ensure the service is running and the port is correct.\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "CrashLoopBackOff errors",
+        "description": "If you see CrashLoopBackOff errors, check the pod logs for errors:\n```bash\nkubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace zot\n# Review the logs for any application errors that may indicate misconfiguration.\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation of Zot will have all pods in the 'zot' namespace running and accessible via the specified port. You should also be able to configure resource limits and access the service through port-forwarding.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add zot https://zotregistry.dev/charts\nhelm repo update",
+        "helm install zot zot/zot --version v2.1.14 --namespace zot --create-namespace",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace zot",
+        "kubectl edit deployment zot --namespace zot\n# Add the following under spec.containers:\n# resources:\n#   limits:\n#     cpu: \"500m\"\n#     memory: \"512Mi\"",
+        "kubectl port-forward service/zot --namespace zot 8080:8080"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "app-definition",
+      "sandbox"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "security-compliance"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Deployment",
+      "Service",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "projectVersion": "v2.1.14",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "ghcr.io/project-zot/zot:v2.1.14"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://zotregistry.dev",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/project-zot/zot"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-02T22:54:46.197Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-install-gen-1.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,140 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-sermant",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Sermant on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Sermant is a proxyless service mesh based on Java bytecode enhancement technology, designed to provide service governance capabilities in microservices architectures. Installing Sermant allows you to enhance your Java applications with governance features without modifying their source code.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the Sermant Helm repository",
+        "description": "Add the Sermant Helm chart repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:\n```bash\nhelm repo add sermant https://sermant-io.github.io/Sermant\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install Sermant using Helm",
+        "description": "Install Sermant with the specified version and create a namespace for it:\n```bash\nhelm install sermant sermant/sermant --version v2.3.0 --namespace sermant --create-namespace\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that the Sermant pods are running:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n sermant\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits",
+        "description": "Set resource limits for Sermant by editing the deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl edit deployment sermant -n sermant\n# Add resource limits under spec.containers:\n# resources:\n#   limits:\n#     memory: \"512Mi\"\n#     cpu: \"500m\"\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Port-forward to access Sermant backend",
+        "description": "Forward a local port to the Sermant backend service to access it:\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward service/sermant-backend 8080:80 -n sermant\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
+        "description": "Uninstall the Sermant release from your cluster:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall sermant --namespace sermant\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)",
+        "description": "Remove any CRDs that were created during the installation:\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd <crd-name>\n# List CRDs to find the names:\nkubectl get crd\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the Sermant namespace",
+        "description": "Delete the namespace to clean up all resources:\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace sermant\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Backup current state",
+        "description": "Take a backup of the current configuration and resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n sermant -o yaml > sermant-backup.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Ensure you have the latest version of the Sermant Helm chart:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Sermant",
+        "description": "Upgrade the Sermant installation to the latest version:\n```bash\nhelm upgrade sermant sermant/sermant --version v2.3.0 --namespace sermant\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
+        "description": "Check that the Sermant pods are running after the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n sermant\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pods stuck in Pending state",
+        "description": "If your pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability:\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n sermant\n# Look for events indicating insufficient resources or scheduling issues.\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Sermant backend not reachable",
+        "description": "If you cannot access the Sermant backend, ensure the service is running:\n```bash\nkubectl get svc -n sermant\n# Check if the backend service is listed and the correct ports are exposed.\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "High memory usage",
+        "description": "If you notice high memory usage, consider adjusting resource limits:\n```bash\nkubectl edit deployment sermant -n sermant\n# Modify the resources section to allocate more memory or CPU.\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation of Sermant will have all pods running in the 'sermant' namespace, and you will be able to access the backend service through the forwarded port. You can also verify the service governance capabilities in your Java applications.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add sermant https://sermant-io.github.io/Sermant\nhelm repo update",
+        "helm install sermant sermant/sermant --version v2.3.0 --namespace sermant --create-namespace",
+        "kubectl get pods -n sermant",
+        "kubectl edit deployment sermant -n sermant\n# Add resource limits under spec.containers:\n# resources:\n#   limits:\n#     memory: \"512Mi\"\n#     cpu: \"500m\"",
+        "kubectl port-forward service/sermant-backend 8080:80 -n sermant"
+      ]
+    }

@@ -0,0 +1,148 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-spinkube",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Spinkube on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Spin Operator is a Kubernetes operator that enables platform engineers to deploy Spin applications as custom resources to their Kubernetes clusters. Installing it allows you to manage Spin applications efficiently within your Kubernetes environment.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Add the Spin Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.\n```bash\nhelm repo add spin-operator https://github.com/spinframework/spin-operator/releases/download/v0.6.1\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install the Spin Operator",
+        "description": "Install the Spin Operator using Helm in a dedicated namespace.\n```bash\nhelm install spin-operator spin-operator/spin-operator --namespace spin-operator --create-namespace --version 0.6.1\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check if the Spin Operator pods are running successfully.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits",
+        "description": "Update the Spin Operator deployment to set resource limits for the operator container.\n```bash\nkubectl patch deployment spin-operator-controller-manager --namespace spin-operator --type='json' -p='[{\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources\", \"value\": {\"limits\": {\"cpu\": \"500m\", \"memory\": \"128Mi\"}, \"requests\": {\"cpu\": \"10m\", \"memory\": \"64Mi\"}}}]'\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy a sample Spin application",
+        "description": "Apply a sample Spin application configuration to test the operator.\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f ./config/samples/simple.yaml --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Port-forward to access the application",
+        "description": "Forward a local port to access the deployed Spin application.\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/simple-spinapp 8083:80 --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
+        "description": "Uninstall the Spin Operator Helm release from the cluster.\n```bash\nhelm uninstall spin-operator --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up Custom Resource Definitions",
+        "description": "Delete the SpinApp and SpinAppExecutor CRDs from the cluster.\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd spinapps.core.spinkube.dev\nkubectl delete crd spinappexecutors.core.spinkube.dev\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the namespace",
+        "description": "Delete the namespace used for the Spin Operator.\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Backup current state",
+        "description": "Export the current configuration and resources for backup.\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace spin-operator -o yaml > backup-spin-operator.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Fetch the latest Helm chart updates.\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade the Spin Operator",
+        "description": "Upgrade the Spin Operator to the latest version.\n```bash\nhelm upgrade spin-operator spin-operator/spin-operator --namespace spin-operator --version 0.6.1\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
+        "description": "Check if the Spin Operator pods are running the new version.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pods stuck in Pending state",
+        "description": "If the Spin Operator pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources in the cluster.\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Spin application not responding",
+        "description": "If the Spin application is not responding, verify the service and pod status.\n```bash\nkubectl get svc --namespace spin-operator\nkubectl get pods --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Helm release failed",
+        "description": "If the Helm release fails, check the Helm release history for errors.\n```bash\nhelm history spin-operator --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "CRDs not found",
+        "description": "If you encounter errors related to CRDs, ensure they are installed correctly.\n```bash\nkubectl get crd --namespace spin-operator\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation of the Spin Operator will show the operator pods running in the 'spin-operator' namespace. You should also be able to access the sample Spin application via the port-forwarded local address.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add spin-operator https://github.com/spinframework/spin-operator/releases/download/v0.6.1\nhelm repo update",
+        "helm install spin-operator spin-operator/spin-operator --namespace spin-operator --create-namespace --version 0.6.1",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace spin-operator",
+        "kubectl patch deployment spin-operator-controller-manager --namespace spin-operator --type='json' -p='[{\"op\": \"replace\", \"path\": \"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources\", \"value\": {\"limits\": {\"cpu\": \"500m\", \"memory\": \"128Mi\"}, \"requests\": {\"cpu\": \"10m\", \"memory\": \"64Mi\"}}}]'",
+        "kubectl apply -f ./config/samples/simple.yaml --namespace spin-operator"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "runtime",
+      "sandbox"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "spinkube"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Deployment",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "projectVersion": "v0.6.1",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "ghcr.io/spinframework/spin-operator:v0.6.1"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://www.spinkube.dev/docs/overview/",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/spinframework/spin-operator",
+      "helm": "https://github.com/spinframework/spin-operator/tree/main/charts/spin-operator/"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "Ensure that Helm and kubectl are installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster."
+  },

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-tremor",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Tremor on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Tremor is an event- or stream-processing system designed for high-volumetric data, providing low latency and efficient resource usage. Installing Tremor allows you to handle ETL workloads effectively, making it a valuable tool for data processing in production environments.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Add the Tremor Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.\n```bash\nhelm repo add tremor https://charts.tremor.rs\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install Tremor",
+        "description": "Install the Tremor Helm chart in a new namespace called 'tremor'.\n```bash\nhelm install tremor tremor/tremor --namespace tremor --create-namespace --version v0.13.0-rc.33\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check if the Tremor pods are running successfully.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace tremor\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits",
+        "description": "Update the Tremor deployment to set resource limits for CPU and memory.\n```bash\nkubectl set resources deployment/tremor --namespace tremor --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Set up port forwarding for access",
+        "description": "Forward a local port to the Tremor service to access it.\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward service/tremor --namespace tremor 8080:80\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
+        "description": "Uninstall the Tremor release from the cluster.\n```bash\nhelm uninstall tremor --namespace tremor\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the Tremor namespace",
+        "description": "Remove the Tremor namespace to clean up all associated resources.\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace tremor\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Backup current state",
+        "description": "Export the current configuration and state of the Tremor deployment.\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace tremor -o yaml > tremor-backup.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Ensure the Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade the Tremor installation",
+        "description": "Upgrade the Tremor release to the latest version.\n```bash\nhelm upgrade tremor tremor/tremor --namespace tremor --version v0.13.0-rc.33\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
+        "description": "Check the status of the Tremor pods to ensure they are running the new version.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace tremor\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pods stuck in Pending state",
+        "description": "If the Tremor pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability.\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace tremor\n``` \nEnsure there are enough resources in the cluster or adjust resource requests/limits."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Tremor service not responding",
+        "description": "If the service is not responding, check the logs of the Tremor pods for errors.\n```bash\nkubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace tremor\n``` \nLook for any error messages that indicate configuration issues."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Helm upgrade fails",
+        "description": "If the Helm upgrade fails, check the Helm release history for errors.\n```bash\nhelm history tremor --namespace tremor\n``` \nYou can rollback to a previous version if necessary using:\n```bash\nhelm rollback tremor <revision-number> --namespace tremor\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation of Tremor will have all pods running in the 'tremor' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via the forwarded port. Resource limits will be configured, ensuring optimal performance.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add tremor https://charts.tremor.rs\nhelm repo update",
+        "helm install tremor tremor/tremor --namespace tremor --create-namespace --version v0.13.0-rc.33",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace tremor",
+        "kubectl set resources deployment/tremor --namespace tremor --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi",
+        "kubectl port-forward service/tremor --namespace tremor 8080:80"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "app-definition",
+      "sandbox"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "tremor"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Deployment",
+      "Service",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "projectVersion": "v0.13.0-rc.33",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "tremor/tremor:v0.13.0-rc.33"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://www.tremor.rs",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/tremor-rs/tremor-runtime"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-02T23:59:03.931Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-install-gen-1.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-volcano",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Volcano on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Volcano is a Kubernetes-native batch scheduling system that enhances the capabilities of the standard kube-scheduler. It is designed to manage and optimize various batch and elastic workloads, making it ideal for AI, machine learning, and big data applications.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the Volcano Helm repository",
+        "description": "First, add the Volcano Helm repository to your local Helm configuration. This allows you to install Volcano using Helm.\n```bash\nhelm repo add volcano https://volcano.sh/volcano\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install Volcano using Helm",
+        "description": "Now, install Volcano with Helm, specifying the namespace and creating it if it doesn't exist.\n```bash\nhelm install volcano volcano/volcano --namespace volcano --create-namespace --version v1.14.1\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that the Volcano pods are running properly in the 'volcano' namespace.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace volcano\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Post-install configuration",
+        "description": "Configure resource limits for the Volcano scheduler to optimize performance. Edit the configuration as needed.\n```bash\nkubectl edit configmap volcano-config --namespace volcano\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Set up RBAC for Volcano",
+        "description": "Ensure that the necessary Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) permissions are set up for Volcano.\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/volcano-sh/volcano/master/docs/rbac/volcano-rbac.yaml --namespace volcano\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the Helm release",
+        "description": "Uninstall the Volcano release using Helm to remove the deployed resources.\n```bash\nhelm uninstall volcano --namespace volcano\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up CRDs",
+        "description": "Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were installed with Volcano.\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep volcano.sh | awk '{print $1}')\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the namespace",
+        "description": "Finally, delete the namespace used for Volcano to clean up any remaining resources.\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace volcano\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Backup current state",
+        "description": "Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of Volcano.\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace volcano -o yaml > volcano-backup.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Ensure that your Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Volcano",
+        "description": "Upgrade your existing Volcano installation to the latest version.\n```bash\nhelm upgrade volcano volcano/volcano --namespace volcano --version v1.14.1\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the upgrade",
+        "description": "Check that the Volcano pods are running the new version properly.\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace volcano\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Pods stuck in Pending state",
+        "description": "If pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability and scheduling issues.\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace volcano\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Volcano scheduler not starting",
+        "description": "If the Volcano scheduler is not starting, check the logs for errors.\n```bash\nkubectl logs <scheduler-pod-name> --namespace volcano\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Resource quota exceeded",
+        "description": "If you encounter resource quota issues, check the resource limits set for the namespace.\n```bash\nkubectl get resourcequota --namespace volcano\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configuration errors",
+        "description": "If there are configuration errors, validate the Volcano configuration map.\n```bash\nkubectl get configmap volcano-config --namespace volcano -o yaml\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful installation of Volcano will have all pods running in the 'volcano' namespace without errors. You can submit batch jobs and monitor their execution through the Volcano scheduler.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add volcano https://volcano.sh/volcano\nhelm repo update",
+        "helm install volcano volcano/volcano --namespace volcano --create-namespace --version v1.14.1",
+        "kubectl get pods --namespace volcano",
+        "kubectl edit configmap volcano-config --namespace volcano",
+        "kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/volcano-sh/volcano/master/docs/rbac/volcano-rbac.yaml --namespace volcano"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration",
+      "cncf",
+      "orchestration",
+      "incubating"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "volcano"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Configmap",
+      "Namespace"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "installation",
+      "configuration"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "incubating",
+    "projectVersion": "v1.14.1",
+    "containerImages": [
+      "volcano.sh/volcano:v1.14.1"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://volcano.sh",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/volcano-sh/volcano"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 100
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "helm",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-03T00:13:01.806Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-install-gen-1.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-wasmedge-runtime",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Wasmedge Runtime on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "WasmEdge is a lightweight, high-performance WebAssembly runtime designed for cloud-native, edge, and decentralized applications. Installing WasmEdge allows you to run serverless apps, microservices, and smart contracts efficiently on various devices.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Add the WasmEdge Helm repository",
+        "description": "First, add the WasmEdge Helm repository to your Helm configuration:\n```bash\nhelm repo add wasmedge https://wasmedge.org/charts\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install WasmEdge using Helm",
+        "description": "Now, install WasmEdge with the following command, creating a namespace if it doesn't exist:\n```bash\nhelm install wasmedge wasmedge/wasmedge --namespace wasmedge --create-namespace --version 0.16.1\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that the WasmEdge pods are running:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods --namespace wasmedge\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure resource limits",
+        "description": "You may want to set resource limits for your WasmEdge deployment. Edit the deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl edit deployment wasmedge --namespace wasmedge\n```\nAdd the following under `spec.containers.resources`:\n```yaml\nresources:\n  limits:\n    memory: \"512Mi\"\n    cpu: \"500m\"\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Set up port forwarding for access",
+        "description": "To access the WasmEdge service, set up port forwarding:\n```bash\nkubectl port-forward svc/wasmedge --namespace wasmedge 8080:80\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the WasmEdge Helm release",
+        "description": "Uninstall the WasmEdge release from your cluster:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall wasmedge --namespace wasmedge\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up CRDs and resources",
+        "description": "Remove any custom resource definitions (CRDs) created by WasmEdge:\n```bash\nkubectl delete crd wasmedge.*\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the WasmEdge namespace",
+        "description": "Finally, delete the WasmEdge namespace to clean up:\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace wasmedge\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Backup current state",
+        "description": "Before upgrading, back up your current configuration and resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get all --namespace wasmedge -o yaml > wasmedge-backup.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Update the Helm repository",
+        "description": "Ensure you have the latest charts from the WasmEdge repository:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade WasmEdge",
